message:
chore(beads): close bd-2vr05.13.4 markers task, start bd-ehk.4 FrankenLab harness

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1021,7 +1021,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.13.1","title":"task(api-events): define deterministic host event taxonomy and ordering contract","description":"Background
- Integrators need deterministic, typed lifecycle events comparable to xterm.js (`onData`, `onBinary`, `onResize`, render/selection/title/bell notifications) but with stricter ordering guarantees.

Goal
- Define a canonical FrankenTermJS host event taxonomy, ordering contract, and failure/backpressure behavior.

Scope
- Enumerate supported events and payload schemas at Rust/JS boundary.
- Specify ordering guarantees across feed/render/reply/selection paths.
- Define listener lifecycle, disposal, and bounded buffering policy.
- Specify structured diagnostic fields per event class.

Why This Matters
- Clear event contracts prevent integration drift and eliminate undocumented behavior dependencies.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- Add unit tests for event schema validation and ordering invariants.
- Add E2E fixtures that assert event sequencing under resize, burst input, and mode transitions.
- Emit structured event logs with correlation IDs for replay.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Event taxonomy + ordering contract is documented and implemented as enforceable tests.
- Deterministic E2E sequencing checks pass across target browsers.
- Structured logs include event type, sequence number, timestamp, and correlation context.","acceptance_criteria":"

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:44.642323958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T18:30:32.267446554Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T18:30:32.267428580Z","close_reason":"Completed deterministic host-event taxonomy/ordering contract with Node+WASM E2E fixture and structured sequence logs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","events","frankenterm","sdk"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:44.642323958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:07.436385548Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.13.2","title":"task(api-events): implement typed event emitter lifecycle with bounded delivery semantics","description":"Background
- Event contract design must be realized with a performant boundary implementation that remains deterministic under load.

Goal
- Implement event emission and listener lifecycle APIs with bounded buffering and explicit drop/backpressure semantics.

Scope
- Implement typed event emitters and subscription handles in WASM/JS boundary.
- Enforce stable ordering from the contract under concurrent feed/render updates.
- Implement listener disposal and memory-safe cleanup guarantees.
- Instrument event pipeline with structured tracing fields.

Why This Matters
- Reliable event delivery is mandatory for production embeddings and analytics hooks.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- Unit tests validate ordering, listener disposal, and bounded queue semantics.
- E2E tests validate event behavior in realistic browser embedding scenarios.
- Structured logs capture queue depth, drop counters, and sequence continuity.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Event emitter implementation satisfies contract invariants under stress.
- Browser E2E scenarios confirm deterministic behavior and no leaks after listener disposal.
- Structured logs are emitted for all event pipeline anomalies and steady-state telemetry.","acceptance_criteria":"

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FrostyGate","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:45.037058054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T15:42:13.857842012Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T15:42:13.857823978Z","close_reason":"Completed: typed host event subscriptions + bounded delivery + tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","events","frankenterm","observability","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:08.211291673Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:45.037058054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:07.822786045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.13.3","title":"task(parser): implement CSI/OSC/DCS/ESC hook APIs with quotas and capability gating","description":"Background
- Advanced terminal apps rely on parser hook extension points (CSI/OSC/DCS/ESC handlers) for domain-specific control flows.

Goal
- Implement explicit parser hook registration APIs with deterministic execution order, quotas, and policy controls.

Scope
- Provide registration/deregistration for supported control-sequence classes.
- Enforce ordering, fallback behavior, and error isolation.
- Add resource quotas/timeouts to prevent host abuse.
- Integrate capability flags so unsupported hooks fail predictably.

Why This Matters
- Parser hooks are a key xterm.js differentiator; we need parity without unbounded risk.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- Unit tests cover hook registration lifecycle, ordering, and isolation/fallback behavior.
- E2E scenarios validate host-defined handlers against real terminal traffic.
- Structured logs capture hook invocation traces, latency, and policy rejections.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Hook APIs support CSI/OSC/DCS/ESC classes with deterministic behavior.
- Resource policy enforcement is validated by tests, including adversarial cases.
- Structured logging provides replay-grade hook execution traces.","acceptance_criteria":"

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FrostyGate","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:45.442265142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T14:56:45.449790873Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T14:56:45.449767009Z","close_reason":"Completed parser hook registration/deregistration for CSI/OSC/DCS/ESC with deterministic ordering, capability gating, quotas/timeouts, panic isolation fallback, and structured replay trace events in ftui-extras parser.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","frankenterm","parser","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:08.981452926Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:45.442265142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:08.593585954Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2vr05.13.4","title":"task(markers): implement marker and decoration primitives with viewport-stable anchors","description":"Background
- Marker/decoration primitives are needed for IDE-like overlays, diagnostics, and workflow UX parity.

Goal
- Implement marker + decoration primitives tied to viewport/scrollback model with deterministic anchor behavior.

Scope
- Marker creation/lifecycle APIs with stable anchor semantics across resize and scrollback compaction.
- Decoration model (inline/line/range) with renderer-safe invalidation behavior.
- Integration points for selection/search overlays without race conditions.
- Structured diagnostics for marker/deco churn and invalidation.

Why This Matters
- Rich terminal UX requires first-class annotation primitives; this is a common migration blocker.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- Unit tests for anchor invariants under scroll, resize, wrap, and compaction.
- E2E tests for marker/deco behavior across interactive workflows.
- Structured logs track anchor moves, invalidations, and render synchronization.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Marker/decoration APIs are stable and deterministic under viewport mutations.
- E2E parity scenarios validate overlay correctness under churn.
- Logging surfaces enough context to debug anchor drift or stale decorations.","acceptance_criteria":"

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FrostyGate","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:45.856362540Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:09:30.405718117Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","frankenterm","markers","renderer","scrollback"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:45.856362540Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:09.377193365Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:09.777374566Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:10.176457101Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.13.5","title":"task(options): implement runtime option/theming mutation API with atomic validation","description":"Background
- Integrators need runtime option updates (theme, cursor, behavior flags, perf controls) without re-instantiation.

Goal
- Implement runtime option mutation/theming API with strict validation and deterministic hot-reconfigure behavior.

Scope
- Typed option schema with safe defaults and capability/profile gating.
- Atomic update semantics and rollback on invalid payloads.
- Renderer + engine synchronization for option changes.
- Structured diagnostics for applied/rejected updates.

Why This Matters
- Runtime reconfiguration is central to production embeddings and migration parity.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- Unit tests for option schema validation, atomic updates, and rollback behavior.
- E2E tests for live reconfigure across backends and accessibility modes.
- Structured logs record option diffs, validation outcomes, and applied capability profile.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runtime option API supports deterministic hot updates with explicit validation failures.
- Cross-backend E2E scenarios confirm consistent behavior after live reconfiguration.
- Structured logs provide complete option-change auditability.","acceptance_criteria":"

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:46.256457841Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:22:16.295417175Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","frankenterm","renderer","sdk","theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:46.256457841Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:10.576838396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.7.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:11.370001954Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:10.972463790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.13.6","title":"task(test): add advanced API compatibility harness (unit + browser E2E + structured diff logs)","description":"Background
- New API parity work must be proven with explicit compatibility evidence, not only implementation confidence.

Goal
- Build a comprehensive compatibility harness that validates advanced host API behavior against xterm.js reference expectations and FrankenTerm invariants.

Scope
- Unit-level contract tests for event ordering, parser hooks, markers/decorations, and runtime options.
- Browser E2E matrix across key supported engines with deterministic fixtures.
- Structured logging pipeline that captures API-level traces for diffing/replay.
- CI integration as release-blocking checks.

Why This Matters
- Ensures API parity claims are verifiable and regression-resistant.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- This task is itself the primary validation gate and must include broad unit + E2E coverage.
- E2E harness must include deliberate fault injection and degraded-capability scenarios.
- Structured logs must support side-by-side behavior diffing and triage.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Compatibility harness covers all advanced API subsystems with passing deterministic fixtures.
- CI executes harness as a release-blocking gate.
- Structured logs include sequence IDs, hook traces, option diffs, and failure diagnostics.","acceptance_criteria":"

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:46.658302475Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T06:21:33.153311208Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","ci","e2e","frankenterm","parity","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.10.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:13.334234126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:46.658302475Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:11.749740191Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:12.155164286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:12.557400483Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:12.952311560Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.13.7","title":"task(docs): publish advanced API migration/capability mapping and troubleshooting guide","description":"Background
- Adoption requires clear migration semantics for advanced APIs, not only base terminal operations.

Goal
- Produce a migration and capability mapping for advanced host APIs from xterm.js concepts to FrankenTermJS primitives.

Scope
- Map key xterm.js advanced APIs/events to FrankenTermJS equivalents and intentional divergences.
- Document capability flags, security constraints, and deterministic guarantees.
- Include troubleshooting guidance tied to structured logs from compatibility harness.

Why This Matters
- Reduces migration friction and support load while preserving intentional architectural differences.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- Include doc-tests or schema checks ensuring documented API signatures match shipped types.
- Validate examples via E2E smoke scripts in CI.
- Ensure documentation references structured log fields used during debugging.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Migration guide is complete for advanced API parity scope with explicit compatibility table.
- Examples compile/run in CI and match current API signatures.
- Logging/troubleshooting sections are actionable and aligned with implemented telemetry.","acceptance_criteria":"

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:47.059403237Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:23:13.299539869Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","docs","frankenterm","migration","sdk"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:47.059403237Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:14.125633722Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -1841,7 +1841,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-ehk.1","title":"Asupersync Cx capability context threading","description":"Thread asupersync::Cx capability context through entire FrankenTUI stack. Every async operation, I/O call, and timer takes &Cx. Enables: cooperative cancellation, deadline propagation, deterministic testing via Lab. Cx-everywhere pattern from FrankenFS. TRACING: span 'cx.propagate' with fields: cx_id, deadline_remaining_us, cancelled. LOG: WARN when Cx cancellation triggers mid-render, ERROR if Cx missing (programming error). METRICS: counter cx_cancellations_total, histogram cx_deadline_remaining_at_use_us.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:07.994413300Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T17:15:20.513840754Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T17:15:20.513822319Z","close_reason":"Implemented Cx capability context module in ftui-core with cooperative cancellation, deadline propagation, LabClock for deterministic testing, child contexts, tracing integration, and metrics counters. 22 tests. Re-exported through ftui facade and prelude.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","capability","cross-crate","cx"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ehk.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ehk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ehk.10","title":"E2E test: FrankenLab deterministic replay of full application","description":"End-to-end test: (1) Run FrankenTUI application in FrankenLab with recorded seed, (2) Record all terminal output and final state, (3) Replay same seed, (4) Assert byte-identical output. Covers: async timer events, keyboard input, resize, animation ticks. Must pass for 100 different seeds. LOGGING SPEC: Assert lab.scenario span. Assert zero divergences. Assert all async operations completed deterministically.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:09.184923907Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:09.184923907Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-crate","e2e","frankenlab","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ehk.10","depends_on_id":"bd-ehk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ehk.3","title":"franken_trace and franken_evidence schema compatibility","description":"Implement suite-wide trace and evidence schemas. franken_trace spans must carry schema_version field. franken_evidence records must be versioned and backward-compatible. CI gate: reader/writer compatibility matrix test (trace from version N must replay on N+1). Evidence ledger JSON format shared across all FrankenSuite projects. TRACING: meta-span 'trace.compat_check' with fields: schema_version, reader_version, writer_version, compatible. LOG: ERROR for schema incompatibility. METRICS: counter trace_compat_failures_total.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:08.255495498Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:53.749875899Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-crate","evidence","franken-trace","schema"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ehk.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ehk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ehk.3","depends_on_id":"bd-xox.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-ehk.4","title":"FrankenLab deterministic testing harness integration","description":"Integration with FrankenLab for deterministic async testing. Seed-controlled scheduling, time injection, event ordering. Lab::run_scenario(seed, scenario) -> deterministic output. Record/replay for debugging non-deterministic failures. Every FrankenTUI test that touches async must use Lab. TRACING: span 'lab.scenario' with fields: scenario_name, seed, event_count, duration_us. LOG: INFO for scenario start/end, WARN for scheduling anomalies. METRICS: counter lab_scenarios_run_total.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:08.374068873Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:53.884448806Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-crate","deterministic","frankenlab","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ehk.4","depends_on_id":"bd-37a.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ehk.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ehk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-ehk.4","title":"FrankenLab deterministic testing harness integration","description":"Integration with FrankenLab for deterministic async testing. Seed-controlled scheduling, time injection, event ordering. Lab::run_scenario(seed, scenario) -> deterministic output. Record/replay for debugging non-deterministic failures.
